verses.1: Don’t be envious of evil men,
verses.3: Through wisdom a house is built;
verses.4: by knowledge the rooms are filled
verses.5: A wise man has great power.
verses.6: for by wise guidance you wage your war,
verses.7: Wisdom is too high for a fool.
verses.8: One who plots to do evil
verses.9: The schemes of folly are sin.
verses.10: If you falter in the time of trouble,
verses.11: Rescue those who are being led away to death!
verses.12: If you say, “Behold, we didn’t know this,”
verses.13: My son, eat honey, for it is good,
verses.14: so you shall know wisdom to be to your soul.
verses.15: Don’t lay in wait, wicked man, against the habitation of the righteous.
verses.16: for a righteous man falls seven times and rises up again,
verses.17: Don’t rejoice when your enemy falls.
verses.18: lest the LORD see it, and it displease him,
verses.19: Don’t fret yourself because of evildoers,
verses.20: for there will be no reward to the evil man.
verses.21: My son, fear the LORD and the king.
verses.22: for their calamity will rise suddenly.
verses.23: These also are sayings of the wise:
verses.24: He who says to the wicked, “You are righteous,”
verses.25: but it will go well with those who convict the guilty,
verses.28: Don’t be a witness against your neighbor without cause.
verses.29: Don’t say, “I will do to him as he has done to me;
verses.30: I went by the field of the sluggard,
verses.31: Behold, it was all grown over with thorns.
verses.32: Then I saw, and considered well.
verses.33: a little sleep, a little slumber,
verses.34: so your poverty will come as a robber
